_comment: Generated by kitsoki persona-qa deck from tools/persona_qa/examples/runs/slidey-architect-review (persona-qa-slidey-architect-review). Re-run the command instead of hand-editing derived scenes.

scenes.narration: This deck was generated deterministically from an existing Persona QA run bundle and its recorded media manifest.
scenes.subtitle: Slidey - Software architect presenting frequently - persona-qa-slidey-architect-review
scenes.title: Slidey Architect Review

scenes.body:
Description: Owns architecture reviews, design proposals, and recurring technical briefings.
Surface preference: deck-as-code authoring with quick local preview
Risk focus: reproducible visuals, diagram fidelity, asset lifecycle, presentation maintenance
Starting surface: Open the generated deck and inspect the JSON source before rendering video.
First question: Can I turn an architecture decision into a polished, rerunnable presentation without hand-editing pixels?
Evidence emphasis: Validated Slidey JSON, playback clips, layout checks, and a bundle suitable for sharing.
Escalation trigger: Any visual that cannot be reproduced, diffed, bundled, or reviewed before a meeting.
Finding bias: Prefer findings that improve repeatable technical communication over one-off polish.
scenes.narration: The deck keeps the persona lens visible so reviewers judge the scenario evidence from the intended point of view.

scenes.caption: Rows are sorted by scenario id for deterministic output.
scenes.narration: The scenario table ties each natural-use task to its outcome, evidence coverage, and playback count.
scenes.title: Recorded scenarios

scenes.body:
Status: ready_with_risks
Summary: For a software architect who presents often, Slidey is strong when the talk must be reproducible, visual, and evidence-backed. The main operational risk is asset and bundle discipline: recorded clips, generated HTML, and source JSON need a tight regeneration habit.
Scenarios: 3
Evidence present: 7/7
Proof evidence: 3
pass: deck-as-code - Slidey JSON is reviewable and diffable enough for architecture decks.
pass: layout-gates - Slidey exposes validation and audit commands that catch common rendering failures before a final video render.
pass: playback-embedding - Recorded rrweb clips can become standalone deck scenes.
pass: visual-vocabulary - Scene types cover diagrams, tables, evidence, and playback without bespoke slide tooling.
warn: asset-lifecycle - Published decks need an explicit convention for deck-local assets and bundled HTML.
scenes.narration: The review scene summarizes bundle readiness without rerunning capture or model work.

scenes.caption:
compose-architecture-deck: validated
reuse-recorded-demo: validated
maintain-deck-as-code: validated
scenes.narration: Findings are grouped by type so a technical reviewer can separate strengths from product risks.
scenes.title: Persona judgement

scenes.caption:

scenes.narration: The media scene carries structured playback references so Slidey can render the recorded journey clips.
scenes.title: Playback manifest

scenes.body:
compose-architecture-deck: slidey-preview / Slidey viewer
reuse-recorded-demo: rrweb-replay / Slidey video scene
maintain-deck-as-code: slidey-validate / Slidey CLI
event slidey-001: Opened the architect-design playback and verified it could anchor a technical presentation.
event slidey-002: Confirmed the playback clip is referenced as deck-local evidence.
event slidey-003: Reviewed the deck-as-code workflow against repeat presentation needs.

scenes.body:
Run bundle: tools/persona_qa/examples/runs/slidey-architect-review
Playback scenes: 3
Regenerate: kitsoki persona-qa deck --run-dir <run-dir> --out <deck.slidey.json>
Publish: commit the deck JSON; bundle it for the product-site gallery when it lives under docs/decks/.
scenes.narration: The deck is a derived artifact. The durable source is the run bundle plus the recorded media manifest.
